subject: Product Management
title: Palm Pre
description: A micro-lesson that makes Palm Pre usable.

hook.text: The Palm Pre had multitasking, wireless charging, and gesture navigation — in 2009. The iPhone didn't get those until years later.

buildup.text: Palm's webOS was praised by reviewers as the most innovative mobile OS since the iPhone. Developers loved it.

discovery.text: But Palm was a small company competing against Apple and Google. It couldn't match their marketing or app ecosystem.

twist.text: Sprint was the exclusive carrier. AT&T had the iPhone. Verizon had Android. Sprint's smaller network limited Palm's reach.

climax.text: HP bought Palm for $1.2 billion, then abandoned webOS within 18 months. A superior product died from insufficient distribution.

punchline.text: The best product doesn't always win. Distribution does.

quiz.question: Why did the Palm Pre fail despite having innovative features?
options: Insufficient distribution — limited carrier, small ecosystem, competing against giants
